content:
Trying to build some habits again. Running during my lunch break. Ab workout when kids are being dropped at school. Journaling (or this or blind writing). Meditation 20-60min. 
The blind writing thing (idk what to call it) is kinda fun. I stare at <something/a flame/a wall/the road/beach> and type on my wireless keyboard without looking at the screen. Then I go back and review the comments with AI. 20min is long enough to flit between the various levels of consciousness; to me, by me, through me, as me. I find it more informative than sitting with my breathe and NOT recording my thoughts that I pass over. Since my memory is so bad I have nothing to review after except my body feelings. This is less pure but it gives more data. I think it's important I do both

Gave the kids the choice of what to do for the school holidays. No one wants to do anything with anyone else. It's quite painful because someone is going to be miserable every day. Idk how to fix this. Do I just treat everyone individually? It's very rare when this family actually feels like a family. It's just a house with parents and children. Is that my fault? I tried but I guess it's not enough. Or maybe it's too much. I don't know. 

Funny thing is that I'm not sad about it. It makes it hard to find my own joy and my why but it's not impossible. That's just the challenge I have to face
